_patch_meta.description: Dimension review patch — C10: 111 group dimension updates (52 confirmed, 25 corrected, 34 newly assigned); 8 Session B findings; 4 Session D pointers

record.finding: Three groups (872-001, 872-002, 872-007) in the faithfulness registry describe divine action — covenantal giving, compassionate giving, and eschatological transformation. The faithfulness registry operates significantly in the Theological/Divine-Human register, not primarily as human character description. Session B should map this divine-human asymmetry and assess whether 'faithfulness' as a registry label adequately captures the term natan's actual inner-being range.

operations.description: Session B pointer — faithfulness: divine-human asymmetry in natan groups

record.finding: The tov word family spans Theological/Divine-Human (divine goodness), Moral/Conscience (human moral character), Spiritual/God-ward (experiential good), and Character/Disposition (Spirit-fruit) — a very wide dimensional range for one term. Session B should analyse whether these are genuinely distinct inner-being phenomena or expressions of a unified category of 'goodness' that the English vocabulary separates but Hebrew integrates.

operations.description: Session B pointer — goodness: wide dimensional range of tov

record.finding: Group 911-002 (tsedeqah) is the only group in C10 explicitly engaging imputed righteousness — moral standing credited to the person by God. Session B should examine the theological significance of credited moral standing as a distinct inner-being category, and how it relates to the possessed moral character described in adjacent groups.

operations.description: Session B pointer — honesty/righteousness: imputed righteousness as distinct inner-being category

record.finding: The functional presentational particles (hen H2005, hinneh H2009, hennah H2008) classified into the innocence registry produce a very wide group range — covering confession, theophany, moral verdict, wisdom reflection, and emotional experience. The innocence registry functions as a container for inner-being disclosure framing. Session B should assess whether this breadth is analytically productive or whether the verse context classifications for these particles should be re-examined.

operations.description: Session B pointer — innocence: presentational particles produce wide group range

record.finding: Group 935-001 (aftharsia — incorruptibility) introduces the eschatological transformation of the person at resurrection — a dimension not well captured by existing vocabulary labels. Assigned Theological/Divine-Human as best available fit. Session B should address whether incorruptibility belongs in the integrity domain or warrants separate treatment, and how eschatological constitution of the person relates to present moral character.

operations.description: Session B pointer — integrity: eschatological incorruptibility as distinct dimension

record.finding: The tahor/taher word family in the purity registry spans ritual cleansing (external covenantal state), moral purification (inner), and divine restorative action. Session B should map how the ritual and moral dimensions relate — and whether the programme treats purity primarily as an inner character quality or as a covenantal status granted and maintained through prescribed practice.

operations.description: Session B pointer — purity: ritual vs moral dimensions of tahor/taher

record.finding: The aletheia word family spans five distinct inner-being dimensions across 10 groups: Spiritual/God-ward, Volitional/Will, Character/Disposition, Theological/Divine-Human, Cognitive/Mind. No single dimension captures the full range. Session B should examine whether 'truthfulness' is the right registry label, and whether the term's biblical range is better described as 'truth-orientation' or another formulation that captures both the character quality and the transformational/pneumatological dimensions.

operations.description: Session B pointer — truthfulness: aletheia family spans five dimensions

record.finding: Group 1216-006 (ya.shar — 'God made man upright, but they have sought out many schemes', Eccl. 7:29) is the only group in C10 explicitly addressing the original moral constitution of the human person and its departure. Session B should treat this as a significant anthropological statement and examine its implications for understanding uprightness as a creational category within the inner-being framework.

operations.description: Session B pointer — uprightness: original moral constitution (Eccl. 7:29)

record.description: Registry 77 is named 'honesty' but all its terms are standard Hebrew righteousness vocabulary (tsaddiq H6662 and tsedeqah H6666). The registry label does not match its actual lexical content. Session D should address whether Registry 77 should be renamed 'righteousness' to accurately reflect its vocabulary, and how this relates to Registry 139 (righteousness, dikaioma G1345).

operations.description: Session D pointer — Registry 77 label mismatch: honesty vs. righteousness vocabulary

record.description: The programme vocabulary has no Emotional/Affective dimension. Multiple groups across C10 — particularly in innocence (930-007: anguish, dread, despair, hope) and goodness (884-003: satisfaction, longing) — describe inner states that resist classification under Moral/Conscience, Spiritual/God-ward, or Character/Disposition. Session D should assess whether the programme vocabulary requires an Emotional/Affective category to adequately map the inner-being landscape.

operations.description: Session D pointer — vocabulary gap: no Emotional/Affective dimension in current vocabulary

record.description: The eschatological transformation of the person (incorruptibility at resurrection, aftharsia G0861 in integrity Reg. 92; ashes to beauty, faint spirit to praise in faithfulness Reg. 60 group 872-007) is not adequately captured by existing dimension labels. Both groups are assigned Theological/Divine-Human as the best available fit. Session D should consider whether the programme needs a dedicated label for eschatological or transformational dimensions of the inner being.

operations.description: Session D pointer — vocabulary gap: eschatological/transformational inner-being dimension

record.description: The aletheia word family spans five distinct inner-being dimensions — the widest dimensional range of any single word family reviewed in C10. This raises the question of whether 'truthfulness' is a sufficiently narrow or accurate registry label for what these terms express in their biblical usage. Session D should address the vocabulary fit and whether a broader label such as 'truth-orientation' or a restructured approach to this registry is warranted.

operations.description: Session D pointer — truthfulness registry label question: aletheia five-dimension range
